failure_modes.text: A culturally homogeneous consensus can hide blind spots that a more diverse interpretive set would surface.
failure_modes.mitigation: Staff the decision team for interpretive range: ensure at least two genuinely different cultural or disciplinary perspectives have veto-grade input, not just attendance.

failure_modes.text: Hierarchy and social comfort can suppress dissenting cross-cultural signals before they affect the decision.
failure_modes.source_quote: multicultural-team-dynamics conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when hierarchy overrides cross-cultural signal from lower-power members.
failure_modes.mitigation: Create explicit dissent protocols (e.g., anonymous pre-meeting input, rotating devil's advocate) so hierarchy cannot filter cross-cultural signals before they reach the decision.

failure_modes.text: Teams can recruit diversity for optics while centralizing the actual decision process so tightly that difference never shapes the outcome.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when leaders recruit diversity for optics but keep the real decision process so centralized that difference never shapes the outcome.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit whether diverse voices actually changed the outcome: after each major decision, trace which inputs shifted the answer and flag any pattern of decorative inclusion.

premortem_questions.text: What interpretation or stakeholder signal would this team miss if everyone shared the same default assumptions?
premortem_questions.source_quote: decision quality depends on surfacing assumptions, interpretations, and stakeholder signals that a homogeneous team would miss

premortem_questions.text: Where is hierarchy or social comfort suppressing information from lower-power members?
premortem_questions.source_quote: hierarchy overrides cross-cultural signal from lower-power members

premortem_questions.text: Are we translating across differences to improve reasoning, or just displaying diversity without changing judgment?
premortem_questions.source_quote: turn diversity into better reasoning rather than symbolic representation

heuristics.text: Design the discussion so different backgrounds surface genuinely different interpretations before consensus forms.
Treat cross-cultural disagreement as information about the system, not as noise to smooth away quickly.
heuristics.source_quote: work crosses functions, cultures, or markets

heuristics.text: Check whether lower-power members have an intact path to influence the final decision.
heuristics.source_quote: authority bias when hierarchy overrides cross-cultural signal from lower-power members

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model on turning diversity into better reasoning and signal detection rather than on symbolic representation language.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
